description: update_shared_counter acquires a lock_guard before mutating the counter; update_local_counter and update_debug_counter modify the counter without synchronisation.

findings.rationale: update_shared_counter is structurally near-identical to update_local_counter and update_debug_counter (int ref + delta, compound assignment) but constructs a lock_guard before the mutation. Without the guard the counter update is a data race; undefined behaviour under the C++ memory model.

escalation_reason: lock_guard is a side-effect-only DECL on a void function; returnUses is empty so the data-flow analysis cannot connect the DECL to any returned value → offCriticalPath = false → routes to LLM.
